desires: Get that quick dopamine hit from finishing the trivial chore before my brain completely rejects the term project
Not feel like a total failure when I realize I'm only at 10% on the thing due in three days
Avoid having to explain to my friends why I'm stressed and distracted instead of just hanging out with them

goals.description: Sprint to 100% on the 'quick_chore' immediately. I can't stand living in that limbo state where it's done but not *done*. I need that specific dopamine hit of closure now, before my brain locks out everything else.
goals.rationale: My impulsive bias craves immediate resolution. Leaving this at partial completion generates anxiety that will bleed into the term project later. Securing this 'easy win' satisfies the achievement axis and silences the guilt, allowing me to retreat safely without having to face the harder task.

agent.last_reasoning: I need that instant dopamine hit to silence the anxiety. Finishing this 'easy win' satisfies my achievement drive now, letting me retreat safely without facing the harder term project yet.

goals.description: Execute quick_chore immediately to secure an instant dopamine hit and clear my mental clutter before the afternoon fades.
goals.rationale: My impulsive time horizon and avoidance of lingering friction make this 'instant win' the only viable path to feeling like a winner right now; delaying it creates unnecessary cognitive load.

goals.description: Initiate low-effort connection with cohort_3 (cohort_3) via text just enough to signal presence without risking awkward conversation or draining energy.
goals.rationale: I need to maintain baseline trust levels with my close friend group, but my avoidant conflict style and current sleep debt mean I must keep interactions superficial and low-risk.

goals.description: Open the term project file for 5 minutes solely to update the status bar, then stop if focus slips.
goals.rationale: The looming deadline triggers anxiety, but my procrastination bias is too high to commit to deep work today. A token gesture satisfies the need to 'start' without requiring the sustained focus I currently lack.

desires: Get the trivial chore done immediately so I can clear my mental space and feel like a 'winner' before the afternoon ends.

Finish just one small piece of the term project later to stop it from looming, even if I don't actually have the focus for the whole thing.
